string_table.name: npnxnsnfns1111101111
string_table.string: Skip Navigation. Hit enter to return to the slide.

slidelink.displaytext: Where are you in your career?
What does this mean as a Leader or Associate?
What and why doing it at Carhartt?

search.slidetext: updated 6/21/16
how to navigate this course need navigation help? resources and transcripts next and previous buttons outline and search volume need navigation help?
let’s focus… whereare youheaded?
in your career?
with your relationships?
with your family?
yourself?

new associate mid-career associate experienced associate where are you on your journey? you’re an associate with experience, either newly joined to carhartt or with a few years under your belt. click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore you’re an associate with a wealth of experience and are further down the path of your career at carhartt. click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore you’re an associate starting out on your career journey and newly hired at carhartt! click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore click to explore you must choose a journey to continue. click to return click to return click to return click to return click to return click to return

download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook you are starting out how are you goingto find: direction focus lasting results

you are mid-career you are: looking for more independence discovering ways to manage multiple priorities focusing on balance download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook

you are well on your way your focus is on the next phase - developing new skills, exploring different paths, and challenges. download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook
no matter where you begin your carharttgrow journey, there are ways to grow. how are you going to take control of your journey? if you’re a leader, how will you help your associates grow?
there are many ways to grow at carhartt

definitions what is performance, development, and growth? meets performance exceeds performance development performance goals growth carharttgrow was developed to help with planning and motivation in all these areas.

performance vs. growth performance is defined as the process of accomplishing an action, task, or function. things we do to get the job done. basic skills and knowledge required for your position. performance goals are entered into halogen. performance is focused on the responsibilities that directly impact carhartt. growth is different impacts associates both in and outside of work. used as a pathway to improve career, work life balance, & focus on personal achievement. performance ratings are not dependant on growth plans.

growth with leaders & associates carharttgrow helps leaders to gain insight into motivations and engagement of associates. carharttgrow helps associates cultivate growth goals to: express a vision for their career. own the process. gain access to resources. apply their new skills. carharttgrow helps you to start the growth conversation!
what is carharttgrow? we heard you. associates want to grow. carharttgrow addresses this need with associates and leaders.

what makes this process different? carharttgrow is a guided conversation between leaders and associates. it makes growth a part of your journey at carhartt. its designed to improve productivity, relationships, and stress management. find your own momentum focus on your goals have meaning in your work strive for a balanced life
it’s time to take ownership of your journey!
what do you need to get started? getting started – what’s your role? are you a leader or an associate? or are you both? let’s explore…

who does what ? fills out the carharttgrow workbook leaders associates introduce carharttgrow to their team remind associates carharttgrow is an option an associate is responsible for filling out the carharttgrow workbook. go back go back go back go back go back go back an associate is responsible for setting up all review meetings after they start working with their leader on their carharttgrow plan. go back go back go back go back go back go back an associate is responsible for taking the carharttgrow training, downloading the workbooks, filling them out, and tracking their own progress. go back go back go back go back go back go back an associate is responsible for researching and compiling a list of resources, training, and ideas to help them attain their growth goals. go back go back go back go back go back go back associates are responsible reaching out to their leader to set up an good time to review their carharttgrow plan. go back go back go back go back go back go back leaders are responsible for accepting meeting notices, not sending them to start the carhartgrow conversation with an associate. go back go back go back go back go back go back an associate is responsible for setting up a meeting with a leader to start the carharttgrow conversation and is in control of the process. go back go back go back go back go back go back leaders are responsible for listening to the priorities and goals that an associate feels comfortable sharing for their carharttgrow plan. go back go back go back go back go back go back leaders are responsible for taking carharttgrow training in order to become familiar with the roles and how to participate in the conversation. go back go back go back go back go back go back an associate is responsible for seeking out tracking their progress to their goals in carharttgrow process. go back go back go back go back go back go back leaders are responsible for coaching and guiding associates to take advantage of tools, resources, and training at carhartt. go back go back go back go back go back go back great job! if you have additional questions about who does what and when for the carharttgrow process, review the roles and responsibilities table in your workbook. continue continue continue continue continue continue leaders are responsible for introducing the carharttgrow program to all existing associates after the program launches in 2016. go back go back go back go back go back go back leaders are responsible for reminding associates on their team that the carharttgrow is an option for growth planning six months after hire or transfer to their team. go back go back go back go back go back go back

timing of carharttgrow new associates can begin six months after their hire date participation is voluntary if you’ve been with carhartt you can begin anytime it’s entirely up to you we recommend planning for the next 12 months of growth

now what? download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook download the carharttgrow workbook

starting the carharttgrow process focus your priorities define your values set your goals gather your tools track your progress document your ideas validate your responses grow an action plan let’s get started…
what do you focus on? work home relationships yourself

work work work work work work home home home home home home relationships relationships relationships relationships relationships relationships self self self self self self meet charles his details: 10 years with carhartt works in it manages 5 associates wants to manage larger teams volunteers with little league what do you think his priorities are? that's right! he wants to focus on managing larger teams as well as put attention toward his little league team – his priorities are work and relationships. continue continue continue continue continue continue because he wants to focus on managing larger teams as well as put attention toward his little league team – his priorities are work and relationships. continue continue continue continue continue continue

work work work work work work home home home home home home relationships relationships relationships relationships relationships relationships self self self self self self meet amy her details: new to carhartt confused on where to start. active runner wants to run a 1/2 marathon what do you think her priorities are? that's right! she wants to focus on her new career (work)as well as have focused time to work on herself. continue continue continue continue continue continue because she wants to focus on her new career as well as have focused time to work on herself– her priorities are work and self. continue continue continue continue continue continue

work work work work work work home home home home home home relationships relationships relationships relationships relationships relationships self self self self self self meet jose his details: new to carhartt butmid-career wants to focus on his family concerned about financialplanning is starting to plan for college what do you think his priorities are? that's right! he wants to focus on his family life and work on planning his kid’s future – his priorities are home and relationships. continue continue continue continue continue continue because he wants to focus on his family life and work on planning his kid’s future – his priorities are home and relationships. continue continue continue continue continue continue

choose your priorites where does your focus go to most often? what priority would you like to give more attention to? do you often achieve balance between the priorities? how do you want to spend your time?
your core values core values guide your behaviors help you determine the right path different types

the team’s core values what values to they focus and base decisons on? click each photo to find out more. charles values expertise and directness at work. with his community work, he likes to focus on optimism and sensitivity, because he works with disadvantaged kids. with his partner – he values fun and enjoys the time he spends outside of work with his family. go back go back go back go back go back go back amy values accountability with her work and is striving for new and different experiences when she can. she also focuses on challenge with her activities with running, and is excited about the training regimen for her half marathon. go back go back go back go back go back go back jose values balance and contentment and is moving toward incorporating this with his efforts at work. he also is focusing on resourcefulness and stability in his home life. he wants to find harmony between work and home. go back go back go back go back go back go back
go through the list of values in your workbook. put a check mark next to the values that are important to you. choose your core values my value
building s.m.a.r.t. goals how are you going to make your goals a reality? s.m.a.r.t.

building s.m.a.r.t. goals click on each nail head to view a thorough explanation of the s.m.a.r.t elements. specific - consider who, what, when, where, why and how in developing the goal. measurable - include a numeric or descriptive measurement. action-oriented - what step will you take to achieve the goal. realistic - what results can realistically be achieved, given available resources. time-bound - it is essential that goals have a timeframe or target date.

s.m.a.r.t. components pecific easurable ctionable ealistic imebound build charles’ s.m.a.r.t. goal build charles’ goal by dragging and dropping each s.m.a.r.t. component to the category it belongs to on the left. charles wants to: improve his emotional intelligence by discovering 6 new tips or techniques by searching for and reading blog posts using his home internet before the new season starts in 6 months great job! (s) charles wants to increase his emotional intelligence by discovering (m) 6 new tips through (a) internet searches using (r) his home internet (t) before the new season starts in 6 months. continue continue continue continue continue continue (s) charles wants to increase his emotional intelligence by discovering (m) 6 new tips through (a) internet searches using (r) his home internet (t) before the new season starts in 6 months. the correct framework is: continue continue continue continue continue continue

s.m.a.r.t. components pecific easurable ctionable ealistic imebound build amy’s s.m.a.r.t. goal build amy’s goal by dragging and dropping each s.m.a.r.t. component to the category it belongs to on the left. amy wants to: gain knowledge of manufacturing from concept to customer delivery by scheduling meetings to chat with others in the company about what they do each month over the next year great job! (s) amy wants to gain knowledge of manufacturing(m) from concept to customer delivery (a) by scheduling meeting to chat(r) with others in the company (t) each month over the next year. continue continue continue continue continue continue the correct framework is: (s) amy wants to gain knowledge of manufacturing(m) from concept to customer delivery (a) by scheduling meeting to chat(r) with others in the company (t) each month over the next year. continue continue continue continue continue continue

s.m.a.r.t. components pecific easurable ctionable ealistic imebound build jose’s s.m.a.r.t. goal build jose’s goal by dragging and dropping each s.m.a.r.t. component to the category it belongs to on the left. jose wants to: spend more time with family by changing one business trip to a virtual meeting using available technology one time per month over the next year great job! (s) jose wants to spend with his family (m) by changing one business trip (a) to a virtual meeting (r) using available technology in the company (t) once a month over the next year. continue continue continue continue continue continue the correct framework is: (s) jose wants to spend with his family (m) by changing one business trip (a) to a virtual meeting (r) using available technology in the company (t) once a month over the next year. continue continue continue continue continue continue

how s.m.a.r.t. is this goal? examine the goal below to identify the missing element. specific specific specific specific specific specific measurable measurable measurable measurable measurable measurable actionable actionable actionable actionable actionable actionable realistic realistic realistic realistic realistic realistic time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound design a new outreach strategy with a vendor and pilot using social media to increase the usage of our mobile phone app by 25% by our customers. correct great job! this goal does not have a time-bound element. to make it s.m.a.r.t., it needs to have a time constraint, such as “within the next six months”. continue continue continue continue continue continue incorrect this goal does not have a time-bound element. to make it s.m.a.r.t., it needs to have a time constraint, such as “within the next six months”. continue continue continue continue continue continue

how s.m.a.r.t. is this goal? examine the goal below to identify the missing element. specific specific specific specific specific specific measurable measurable measurable measurable measurable measurable actionable actionable actionable actionable actionable actionable realistic realistic realistic realistic realistic realistic time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound create a list of 100 targeted potential hires for the sales department and connect with 25% of them by 3rd quarter. correct that's right! this goal lacks a realistic component illustrating a tool or resource that will help attain the goal. continue continue continue continue continue continue incorrect this goal lacks a realistic component illustrating a tool or resource that will help attain the goal. continue continue continue continue continue continue

how s.m.a.r.t. is this goal? examine the goal below to identify the missing element. specific specific specific specific specific specific measurable measurable measurable measurable measurable measurable actionable actionable actionable actionable actionable actionable realistic realistic realistic realistic realistic realistic time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound time-bound to donate blood over the next year by participating in blood drives offered on site by red cross. correct that's right! this goal lacks a measurable component – for example, to donate blood 6 times within the year long period. continue continue continue continue continue continue incorrect this goal lacks a measurable component – for example, to donate blood 6 times within the year long period. continue continue continue continue continue continue
set your goals work learning take facilitation skills training to improve my presenting skills 2016 3 qtr

gathering tools and resources identifying and cultivating a relationship with a mentor/role model. reading relevant material. engaging in training and education. keeping a journal. attending appropriate seminars. take on special job assignments or job rotation. receive coaching from a skilled co-worker. incorporate activities into ongoing work assignments. reaching out to community organizations. start by making a list of opportunities

charles wants to help his little league team by improving his emotional intelligence. what do you suggest he explore? help charles gather tools check out books on emotional intelligence from carhartt university. check out books on emotional intelligence from carhartt university. check out books on emotional intelligence from carhartt university. check out books on emotional intelligence from carhartt university. check out books on emotional intelligence from carhartt university. check out books on emotional intelligence from carhartt university. take an emotional intelligence class from carhartt learning. take an emotional intelligence class from carhartt learning. take an emotional intelligence class from carhartt learning. take an emotional intelligence class from carhartt learning. take an emotional intelligence class from carhartt learning. take an emotional intelligence class from carhartt learning. book time with a speaker on motivation. book time with a speaker on motivation. book time with a speaker on motivation. book time with a speaker on motivation. book time with a speaker on motivation. book time with a speaker on motivation. lead a discussion group on positivity. lead a discussion group on positivity. lead a discussion group on positivity. lead a discussion group on positivity. lead a discussion group on positivity. lead a discussion group on positivity. explore blogs on motivating adults. explore blogs on motivating adults. explore blogs on motivating adults. explore blogs on motivating adults. explore blogs on motivating adults. explore blogs on motivating adults. good job! that's right! checking out books on emotional intelligence from the corporate library or taking a course from carhartt learning are the best choices for charles. continue continue continue continue continue continue there are better choices: checking out books on emotional intelligence from the corporate library or taking a course from carhartt learning are the best choices for charles. continue continue continue continue continue continue

amy would like to learn more about the manufacturing process from concept to consumer delivery. what do you suggest she explore? help amy gather tools talking to her fellow associates. talking to her fellow associates. talking to her fellow associates. talking to her fellow associates. talking to her fellow associates. talking to her fellow associates. attending department spotlights attending department spotlights attending department spotlights attending department spotlights attending department spotlights attending department spotlights sending a mass email out to the company sending a mass email out to the company sending a mass email out to the company sending a mass email out to the company sending a mass email out to the company sending a mass email out to the company attend networking meeting outside of work attend networking meeting outside of work attend networking meeting outside of work attend networking meeting outside of work attend networking meeting outside of work attend networking meeting outside of work reading common thread reading common thread reading common thread reading common thread reading common thread reading common thread good job! amy can learn about the manufacturing process by talking to co-workers that are in that role, attending associated company spotlights, and reading articles featuring manufacturing processes in common thread. continue continue continue continue continue continue there are better choices: amy can learn about the manufacturing process by talking to co-workers that are in that role, attending associated company spotlights, and reading articles featuring manufacturing processes in common thread. continue continue continue continue continue continue

jose wants to reduce his travel time by utilizing technology for virtual meetings. what do you suggest he explore? help him test available technology help him test available technology help him test available technology help him test available technology help him test available technology help him test available technology suggest researching video tools on the internet suggest researching video tools on the internet suggest researching video tools on the internet suggest researching video tools on the internet suggest researching video tools on the internet suggest researching video tools on the internet talk to accounting about options talk to accounting about options talk to accounting about options talk to accounting about options talk to accounting about options talk to accounting about options take a course on running virtual meetings effectively take a course on running virtual meetings effectively take a course on running virtual meetings effectively take a course on running virtual meetings effectively take a course on running virtual meetings effectively take a course on running virtual meetings effectively take a course on managing virtual teams take a course on managing virtual teams take a course on managing virtual teams take a course on managing virtual teams take a course on managing virtual teams take a course on managing virtual teams help jose gather tools good job! jose can use your help testing technology, talk to accounting, and take courses on managing virtual teams or running virtual meetings. continue continue continue continue continue continue there are better choices: jose can use your help testing technology, talk to accounting, and take courses on managing virtual teams or running virtual meetings. continue continue continue continue continue continue

your tools and resources what priorities are important to you? are your goals reasonable? do they recommend a different path? what roadblocks might be in your way? what practice opportunities are there for you at carhartt in the coming year? what resources do you already have?
staying on track my goal can be accomplished by step one step two step three i need to measure my progress!

the team’s progress measuring your progress is important if you want to get positive emotional effects. click each photo to find out more about how jose, amy, and charles tracked their progress. amy tracked her progress learning about the organization over the course of a year. she met her goal of meeting with one department per month, and tracked her progress on her tracker and is feeling more confident in her role. go back go back go back go back go back go back charles improved his emotional intelligence over the course of the little league season and implemented 7 new tips when working with disadvantaged kids, which he marked on his tracker. his team is progressing both in baseball and with their new life skills. go back go back go back go back go back go back jose successfully converted 10 out of 12 meetings to virtual session over the course of a year and tracked his progress on his tracker. with his additional time with his family, he now gets to schedule more bonding time activities, and develop strong bonds at home. go back go back go back go back go back go back
leader leader leader leader leader leader associate associate associate associate associate associate the carharttgrow conversation

remember amy? amy is taking part in carharttgrow to progress at work and make time to compete in the 1/2 marathon. amy takes the training. she fills out her priorities and values. create s.m.a.r.t. goals create s.m.a.r.t. goals create s.m.a.r.t. goals create s.m.a.r.t. goals create s.m.a.r.t. goals create s.m.a.r.t. goals meet with her leader meet with her leader meet with her leader meet with her leader meet with her leader meet with her leader amy has a few more steps in the carharttgrow process to complete before she meets with her leader. go back go back go back go back go back go back

jose’s part in carharttgrow set up meetings with all his associates to work through the workbook together. set up meetings with all his associates to work through the workbook together. set up meetings with all his associates to work through the workbook together. set up meetings with all his associates to work through the workbook together. set up meetings with all his associates to work through the workbook together. set up meetings with all his associates to work through the workbook together. introduce the program to his team and advise that they take training when they can. introduce the program to his team and advise that they take training when they can. introduce the program to his team and advise that they take training when they can. introduce the program to his team and advise that they take training when they can. introduce the program to his team and advise that they take training when they can. introduce the program to his team and advise that they take training when they can. introduce the carharttgrow process with all new associates at the 6 month mark. introduce the carharttgrow process with all new associates at the 6 month mark. introduce the carharttgrow process with all new associates at the 6 month mark. introduce the carharttgrow process with all new associates at the 6 month mark. introduce the carharttgrow process with all new associates at the 6 month mark. introduce the carharttgrow process with all new associates at the 6 month mark. count on his associates to know about the program and to ask him questions if needed. count on his associates to know about the program and to ask him questions if needed. count on his associates to know about the program and to ask him questions if needed. count on his associates to know about the program and to ask him questions if needed. count on his associates to know about the program and to ask him questions if needed. count on his associates to know about the program and to ask him questions if needed. as a leader, what is jose's first step in the carharttgrow process? great job! that's right! jose should introduce the process to his existing team and remind each new associate 6 months after their start date that the program is available to them. continue continue continue continue continue continue there’s a better option: jose should introduce the process to his existing team and remind each new associate 6 months after their start date that the program is available to them. continue continue continue continue continue continue

jose’s first carharttgrow meeting jose receives a meeting request from amy to discuss her carharttgrow plan. he: declines it. declines it. declines it. declines it. declines it. declines it. forwards it to her hr business partner. forwards it to her hr business partner. forwards it to her hr business partner. forwards it to her hr business partner. forwards it to her hr business partner. forwards it to her hr business partner. accepts it. accepts it. accepts it. accepts it. accepts it. accepts it. invites other to attend. invites other to attend. invites other to attend. invites other to attend. invites other to attend. invites other to attend. great job! because the associate has reached out for a carharttgrow conversation, it’s the leader’s responsibility to make time to review their goals and help the associate find resources. continue continue continue continue continue continue there’s a better option: because the associate has reached out for a carharttgrow conversation, it’s the leader’s responsibility to make time to review their goals and help the associate find resources. continue continue continue continue continue continue

how will jose prepare? refresh his understanding of carharttgrow refresh his understanding of carharttgrow refresh his understanding of carharttgrow refresh his understanding of carharttgrow refresh his understanding of carharttgrow refresh his understanding of carharttgrow get up to speed on amy’s current projects get up to speed on amy’s current projects get up to speed on amy’s current projects get up to speed on amy’s current projects get up to speed on amy’s current projects get up to speed on amy’s current projects look for opportunities for amy to work towards look for opportunities for amy to work towards look for opportunities for amy to work towards look for opportunities for amy to work towards look for opportunities for amy to work towards look for opportunities for amy to work towards brush up on coaching tactics brush up on coaching tactics brush up on coaching tactics brush up on coaching tactics brush up on coaching tactics brush up on coaching tactics before the meeting, what can jose do to prepare? great job! that's right! the leader should brush up on the carharttgrow process and make use of the coaching resources available from carhartt learning. continue continue continue continue continue continue there’s a better option: the leader should brush up on the carharttgrow process and make use of the coaching resources available from carhartt learning. continue continue continue continue continue continue

true true true true true true false false false false false false jose can require amy to share all her priorities and goals with him. it is not required for an associate to share all their goals with their leader. associates should only share the goals they feel comfortable with and that they feel their leader can offer support to achieve them. continue continue continue continue continue continue it is not required for an associate to share all their goals with their leader. associates should only share the goals they feel comfortable sharing and that they feel their leader can help them with. continue continue continue continue continue continue

how can jose help amy? suggest people to talk to about their role. suggest people to talk to about their role. suggest people to talk to about their role. suggest people to talk to about their role. suggest people to talk to about their role. suggest people to talk to about their role. have her present her ideas to the team. have her present her ideas to the team. have her present her ideas to the team. have her present her ideas to the team. have her present her ideas to the team. have her present her ideas to the team. have her attend spotlights. have her attend spotlights. have her attend spotlights. have her attend spotlights. have her attend spotlights. have her attend spotlights. what are some resources that jose can suggest to help amy reach her goal? suggest she become a vitality champ. suggest she become a vitality champ. suggest she become a vitality champ. suggest she become a vitality champ. suggest she become a vitality champ. suggest she become a vitality champ. adjust the time of her shift. adjust the time of her shift. adjust the time of her shift. adjust the time of her shift. adjust the time of her shift. adjust the time of her shift. amy’s goal is to improve understanding of manufacturing processes. by suggesting that she reach out to others in the organization as well as attending department spotlights, jose has provided resources and tools for amy to reach her goal. continue continue continue continue continue continue jose has suggested that she reach out to others in the organization as well as attending department spotlights, which will help amy to reach her goal of learning more about the organization. continue continue continue continue continue continue

what’s the next step? requiring her to write more goals requiring her to write more goals requiring her to write more goals requiring her to write more goals requiring her to write more goals requiring her to write more goals committing to help amy committing to help amy committing to help amy committing to help amy committing to help amy committing to help amy amy has as many goals as she feel comfortable with. she is ready to go onto the next step. go back go back go back go back go back go back

allowing for timeto meet. helping amy overcome barriers. providing introductions to others in the organization. he accepts her 30 minute check in once a month for the rest of year. jose commits to helping amy

yes yes yes yes yes yes no no no no no no i will run three times each week after work until my 1/2 marathon, working with my leader to leave work on time to attain this goal. i will track my runs with mapmyrun app to be accurate. is amy’s goal s.m.a.r.t.? yes, amy’s goal is structured with all the components of s.m.a.r.t. she will track her runs with mapmyrun app to be accurate (bonus - shes tracking her progress). continue continue continue continue continue continue amy’s goal is s.m.a.r.t. she will run (s) three times (m) each week after work (a) until my 1/2 marathon (t), working with my leader to leave work on time (r) to attain this goal. she will track her runs with mapmyrun app to be accurate (bonus - shes tracking her progress). continue continue continue continue continue continue

she starts signing up for all the training she can find. she starts signing up for all the training she can find. she starts signing up for all the training she can find. she starts signing up for all the training she can find. she starts signing up for all the training she can find. she starts signing up for all the training she can find. she shares her plan with her fellow associates. she shares her plan with her fellow associates. she shares her plan with her fellow associates. she shares her plan with her fellow associates. she shares her plan with her fellow associates. she shares her plan with her fellow associates. she sets up a meeting with her leader. she sets up a meeting with her leader. she sets up a meeting with her leader. she sets up a meeting with her leader. she sets up a meeting with her leader. she sets up a meeting with her leader. she posts her goals in her work area. she posts her goals in her work area. she posts her goals in her work area. she posts her goals in her work area. she posts her goals in her work area. she posts her goals in her work area. amy’s carharttgrow process the next step in the carharttgrow process is for amy to meet with her leader to discuss her goals and how they can help her reach them. continue continue continue continue continue continue the next step in the carharttgrow process is for amy to meet with her leader to discuss her goals and how they can help her reach them. continue continue continue continue continue continue

amy reaches out to her leader to discuss her carharttgrow plan and sets up a meeting. what should she do to prepare? enter her growth plan into the performance goals systems at carhartt. enter her growth plan into the performance goals systems at carhartt. enter her growth plan into the performance goals systems at carhartt. enter her growth plan into the performance goals systems at carhartt. enter her growth plan into the performance goals systems at carhartt. enter her growth plan into the performance goals systems at carhartt. copy her carharttgrow plan to show her leader. copy her carharttgrow plan to show her leader. copy her carharttgrow plan to show her leader. copy her carharttgrow plan to show her leader. copy her carharttgrow plan to show her leader. copy her carharttgrow plan to show her leader. create an agenda of the things she’d like to discuss. create an agenda of the things she’d like to discuss. create an agenda of the things she’d like to discuss. create an agenda of the things she’d like to discuss. create an agenda of the things she’d like to discuss. create an agenda of the things she’d like to discuss. share her goals with her fellow associates. share her goals with her fellow associates. share her goals with her fellow associates. share her goals with her fellow associates. share her goals with her fellow associates. share her goals with her fellow associates. amy should come prepared with an agenda of what she would like to discuss with her leader. continue continue continue continue continue continue amy should come prepared with an agenda of what she would like to discuss with her leader. continue continue continue continue continue continue

true true true true true true false false false false false false it is required that amy share all her growth plans with her leader. it is not required to share all your goals with your leader. share only the goals you feel comfortable sharing that leader can help you attain. continue continue continue continue continue continue incorrect it is not required to share all your goals with your leader. share only the goals you feel comfortable sharing that leader can help you attain. continue continue continue continue continue continue

during amy’s meeting, her manager suggests attending spotlights to learn more about the business. jose asks if she would like to explore more options. amy is very shy, so she asks for: personal introductions to others in organization. personal introductions to others in organization. personal introductions to others in organization. personal introductions to others in organization. personal introductions to others in organization. personal introductions to others in organization. presentation skills training. presentation skills training. presentation skills training. presentation skills training. presentation skills training. presentation skills training. early start to her workday. early start to her workday. early start to her workday. early start to her workday. early start to her workday. early start to her workday. dues for an association membership. dues for an association membership. dues for an association membership. dues for an association membership. dues for an association membership. dues for an association membership. correct amy asks for her leader’s help overcoming her shyness by having them provide introductions to others around the organization. continue continue continue continue continue continue incorrect amy asks for her leader’s help overcoming her shyness by having them provide introductions to others around the organization. continue continue continue continue continue continue

amy’s carharttgrow process adjust her personal goals adjust her personal goals adjust her personal goals adjust her personal goals adjust her personal goals adjust her personal goals set up check in meetings set up check in meetings set up check in meetings set up check in meetings set up check in meetings set up check in meetings amy does not have to adjust her goals at this point. she is now ready to take the next step. go back go back go back go back go back go back

amy’s carharttgrow process amy shares her progress. jose shares his feedback. amy also shares her successes and barriers. amy completes all meetings over the course of the year. she also completed her 1/2 marathon with her best time. at the end of the year, amy and jose will meet again with a new carharttgrow workbook to begin the process again. go back and try another role go back and try another role go back and try another role go back and try another role go back and try another role go back and try another role

remember, carharttgrow is voluntary personal flexible driven by the associate it’s in your control. you put in the time, you put in the effort. make it as challenging or rewarding as you want. your growth journey is in your hands. it’s time to start.

additional tools and support post to yammer with the tag #carharttgrow. visit the carharttgrow site. reach out to carhartt learning. download copies of the carharttgrow worksheet, click the resources tab.
complete the surveyª complete the surveyª complete the surveyª complete the surveyª complete the surveyª complete the surveyª

resource_data.description: Here are some useful links and documents:

resource.title: Submit a ticket for technical support

slidetranscripts.slidetranscript: Welcome to CARHARTTGROW!

Need some help with Navigation or Player Controls?

Here’s some instructions …

• In the upper right corner, click the Resource tab to access additional information about this course.

• If you need to view a transcript of the audio, click the Transcript tab.

• Use the Next and Previous buttons in the lower right to navigate back and forth through the course.

• Use the Outline on the left to skip around, but remember - this module is best when experienced start to finish.

• There is Search functionality - just type a term to search for in the text field and then tap the Enter key on your keyboard.

• Finally, adjust the Volume using the slider in the lower right.

If you experience any difficulties while using this module, please reach out to Carhartt Learning or the Help Desk.

Click the Previous button to return to the module.

Let’s focus on you for a moment.

How often have you taken the time to think about where you’re headed?
In your career?
With your relationships?
With your family?
What about yourself?

Where are you at in your journey?

Maybe you’re a new Carhartt Associate, just starting out. You’re looking for ways to grow and change, but are unsure of where to start.

Or you’re a seasoned Associate, well on your way and are looking for ways to continue growing both within the company and beyond.

What’s your journey? Find your path by using the slider.

Drag the handle to the sprout that best defines you to explore your growth options at Carhartt.

You’re starting out with your journey – how are you going to find direction, focus, and lasting results?

Crafting your own personal growth path really isn’t hard if you know where to start. The CARHARTTGROW program helps with independent decision making, planning, finding work/life balance, and forming personal and professional goals.

Let’s begin! Download and print the CARHARTTGROW workbook to fill out during the module. Each section features guided instruction, helping you to complete the CARHARTTGROW plan.

You are mid-career. You have different perspectives and motivations. Whether you are looking for more independence, ways to manage multiple priorities, or focus more on balance, CARHARTTGROW is here to guide you.

Let’s begin! Download and print the CARHARTTGROW workbook to fill out during the module. Each section features guided instruction, helping you to complete the CARHARTTGROW plan.

You are well on your way with your career. Your focus may be on how to transition to the next phase in your journey, developing new skills to explore different paths, or discovering different challenges to be engaged.

Let’s begin! Download and print the CARHARTTGROW workbook to fill out during the module. Each section features guided instruction, helping you to complete the CARHARTTGROW plan.

No matter where you’re at in your journey, there are ways to grow both personally and professionally.

Here’s the big question – how are you going to take control of the process? How will you take advantage of resources and tools available to you? And if you are a Leader – how will you help your Associates grow?

CARHARTTGROW was designed to help answer these questions. Let’s take a closer look…
There are many ways to grow at Carhartt – let’s break down exactly what growth means.

A lot of words describe how an Associate improves or is measured regarding their self or their work.

To put a picture to it, at Carhartt, we have performance evaluations where your performance either meets or exceeds expectations.

First, you develop strong roots and meet performance expectations. To help you meet your goals, sometimes development plans are put in place.

Then you reach for the sky. To move from meets to exceeds, you write goals with your Leader and achieve these goals.

But growth – where does that fit in?

Your growth journey doesn’t just focus on work – there’s also yourself, home, and relationships. Growth is upward and outward, overlapping these areas of your life . CARHARTTGROW was developed to help you with direction and serve as a source of planning and motivation in all these areas.

Let’s dig a little deeper and look at performance vs. growth.

Performance is defined as the process of accomplishing an action, task, or function. These are the things we do at Carhartt to get the job done with our teams every day. Basic skills and knowledge are required to perform these assigned actions or functions. Goals are entered into Halogen that are based on these actions; for projects, strategic plans, and metrics that each of us are evaluated on.

The common theme for performance is that it is solely focused on the responsibilities you have as an Associate that directly impact Carhartt.

Growth is different. Growth impacts Associates both in and outside of work. If you choose to grow as an Associate at Carhartt, it’s a personal endeavor used as a pathway to improve your career, work/life balance, or help you focus on a more personal achievement. As such, performance ratings are not dependent upon your growth plan.

As a Leader, working with Associates on growth goals allows you to gain insight into the motivations and level of engagement of your Associates. Even though some of their goals may not be directly about their work at Carhartt, this is an investment in the Leader/Associate relationship.

As Associates, cultivating personal and professional growth goals with your Leader helps you to express your vision for yourself at Carhartt. You own the process to gain access to resources, foster relationships, and discover ways to apply your growing skills in and outside of Carhartt.

CARHARTTGROW helps start the growth conversation - for Leaders and Associates.

So, what is CARHARTTGROW?

We heard you. Associates want to grow. Often, growth planning is a random process based on work interests rather than on the Associate’s priorities both in and outside of work.

Carhartt Associates and Leaders have asked for a guide to start the growth conversation, help focus everyone’s efforts, and result in a usable tool to track progress.

CARHARTTGROW was developed to address this need.

What makes this process different?

CARHARTTGROW is a guided, personalized conversation between Leaders and Associates. It offers a meaningful approach to making growth a part of your journey. Here at Carhartt, we want to improve productivity, relationships, and stress management for a lasting life and work balance, not solely work performance.

CARHARTTGROW is your opportunity to work with your Leader to cultivate balance and purpose.

CARHARTTGROW guides you to:

· Find your own momentum

· Focus on your goals

· Have meaning in your work

· Strive for a balanced life

By using these planning tools and coaching to focus on the experience, you take ownership of the process.

There you have it. CARHARTTGROW is your chance to uncover and define the unique priorities, values, and goals that will help you grow.

Now, it’s time to take ownership of your CARHARTTGROW journey.

So what do you need to do to get started?

First, define your role to determine your path.

Are you a Leader? Or an Associate? Or are you both?

There are different tasks and responsibilities for Leaders and Associates – let’s explore each one…

Let’s play a quick game.

Here are the tasks that Associates and Leaders must do to drive the CARHARTTGROW process.

Select each task and drag it to the person you think would that role or responsibility.

If you are right, it will drop in the basket, if not feedback will appear with an explanation.

Click Submit when you are done sorting to move forward.

What about the timing of CARHARTTGROW?

If you are a new Associate, the best time to start the CARHARTTGROW process is after the first six months of employment. Participation however, is voluntary and if you want to start later, it is entirely up to you.

If you’ve been an Associate with Carhartt for some time – you may begin with CARHARTTGROW any time you wish or not participate if you choose. Again, it’s entirely up to you.

For Associates, we recommend planning goals for no more than then next 12 months and meeting with your Leaders every quarter to review your progress.

Now you are familiar with who does what and when for Associates and Leaders, let’s take a deep dive into the CARHARTTGROW workbook.

If you need additional support with the Roles and Responsibilities, check out the list included in your Workbook.

Starting the CARHARTTGROW Process

Planning your goals can be intimidating. This workbook is designed to guide you through rest of this module with activities to help you:

· Focus your priorities

· Define your values

· Set your goals

· Gather your tools

· Track your progress

Each one helps you figure out the key points of what makes you unique. You’ll document your ideas, validate your responses, then grow an action plan using the CARHARTTGROW planning guide on the last page of your workbook.

It’s that easy. Let’s get started…

Like a weathervane that shows direction of the wind – your focus swings to the things you prioritize. The important things in your life that define who you are.

At Carhartt, an Associate grows both professionally and personally. We believe that growth is focused on the following priorities:

• Work

• Home

• Relationships

• Yourself

At times, our focus is strong on one priority, sometimes it swings to others. All your priorities combined help you to achieve work life balance.

Let me introduce you to Charles.

Charles has been with Carhartt for over 10 years in the IT department. He manages a team of 5 Associates but wants to explore managing larger teams. He also is very active in his community, volunteering his time with a little league team alongside his partner, who runs the booster club.

What do you think his priorities are? Make your selections, then click Submit to review.

Now, meet Amy.

Amy is new to Carhartt. As a new graduate, she is excited to progress in her career but she is confused on where to start. Also, Amy is very active, participating in 5ks several times a year and is planning on running a 1/2 marathon at the end of the year.

What do you think her priorities are? Make your selections and click Submit to review.

Now meet Jose -

Jose has joined Carhartt mid-career. He made the change because he wanted to move his family to a slower paced neighborhood and focus his extra time on the new addition to his family. He is also concerned about his financial well-being for him and his family and is planning for his kid’s college fund.

What do you think his priorities are? Make your selections and click Submit to review.

Ok - take a moment and ask yourself, for the four priorities:

· Where does your focus go to most often?

· What priority would you like to give more attention to?

· Do you often achieve balance between the priorities?

· How do you want to spend your time?

Take out your workbook and begin to think about these questions. Fill in the priorities you find most important today – but keep in mind this may change as time goes on. Choose from Work, Relationships, Home, or Yourself – or fill in your own priority. We recommend that you keep your list to no more than four – but if you only have one or two priorities - that’s ok too.

Your Core Values

You are constantly making important decisions about your future.

These decisions are made based on your core values, or principles that guide your behavior.

Core values help people to determine if they are on the right path and help fulfill their goals.

There are many different types and examples of core values depending upon the context.

Let’s go back to Charles, Amy, and Jose -

Now that we know their priorities – what about these priorities do they focus on? What core values do they base decisions on?

Click on each photo to learn more about their CARHARTTGROW journey. Click Next to move on with the module.

What core value do you want to follow for each priority?

· Go through the list of values in your workbook.

· Put a check mark next to the values that are important to you. Mark as many as you wish.

· Review the list of values you checked, and pick one for each of your priorities to focus on.

Feel free add your own values if you don’t see them on the list.

So, what’s next?

How are you planning to make your goals a reality? Building goals is easier than you think. A useful acronym to remember is S.M.A.R.T

This framework brings structure and trackability to your goals, allowing you to quickly clarify your path to success.

Play the video to review a brief introduction, then click Next to continue the module.

Ok, now that you have an idea of the elements of the S.M.A.R.T. framework, it’s time to dive a little deeper.

Click on each nail head to view a thorough explanation of each element, then click Next to continue with the module.

So, how are Charles, Amy, and Jose going to build S.M.A.R.T. goals to help them with their priorities?

Let’s start with Charles. Remember, he wants to help his little league team be more positive.

Select a component from the list on the right and drag it to the category you think it fits best to build his S.M.A.R.T. goal on the left. Click Submit to check the framework.

Let’s move on to Amy. She would like to learn more about the manufacturing process from concept to consumer delivery.

elect a component from the list on the right and drag it to the category you think it fits best to build her S.M.A.R.T. goal on the left. Click Submit to check the framework.

Now it’s time for Jose. He wants to reduce his travel time by utilizing technology for virtual meetings.

He is targeting changing one trip a month to a virtual session over the next year. This will allow him to be home to participate in family activities.

Select a component and drag it to the category you think it fits best. Click Submit to check the framework.

OK – now it’s time to practice a bit. How S.M.A.R.T. is this goal?

Examine the goals on the next few slides to identify the missing element.

Choose the category that is missing and click Submit to receive feedback.

Now, it’s time to set your goals for the next year.

By turning your priorities and values into specific and measurable milestones, you develop a path to success.

Here’s a tip: make sure each goal has a deadline, is written in a present tense with action verbs, and written in a positive voice.

Take a moment to sketch out some goals for your each of your priorities in your workbook. Remember, we recommend you set goals for only the next year to make them more attainable.

Now that you have your goals documented, it’s time to determine what kind of support you’ll need.

Explore our list by using the scroll bar to read suggestions on what you can use here at Carhartt and beyond.

Then start building your own list of opportunities you would like to engage in, both at work and personally that helps you to explore achieving your goals.

Let’s help Charles gather his tools and resources. He wants to help his little league team be more positive.

Charles asks you for help with his goals – what do resources do you suggest he explore to improve his emotional intelligence?

Select some options from the list and click Submit to review.

Amy would like to learn more at work about the manufacturing process from concept to consumer delivery.

Amy asks you for help with her goals – what do resources do you suggest she explore?

Select some options from the list and click Submit to review.

Jose wants to reduce his travel time by utilizing technology for virtual meetings.

Jose asks you for help with his goals – what do resources do you suggest he explore?

Select some options from the list and click Submit to review.

What tools and resources do you need? After you build your list, your Leader is the next resource you should tap into to achieve your goals.

Once you have your gathered your ideas, schedule time with your Leader to discuss:

· What priorities are important to you.

· Are your goals reasonable?

· Do they recommend a different path?

· What roadblocks might be in your way?

· What practice opportunities are there for you at Carhartt in the coming year?

· What resources do you already have?

Use this opportunity to have your Leader provide guidance, coaching, and support. Remember, you don’t have to share all your goals with them, but if you feel like they can be of help to your specific priorities, let them know.

Staying on Track

There is little point in setting a goal if you cannot determine success. When you measure your progress, you’ll find powerful motivation to help you stay on track.

Think about the actions you need to perform in order to reach your goals. Can you break them down into manageable steps, so it’s easy for you to see the next step forward? Then you can look back to see how far you’ve come. Use these milestones to track your progress.

Use the attached tracker to check off your progress each week to your goals. Here’s a tip: set up a reminder on your Outlook calendar to do this once a week so you don’t miss out on this valuable self-reflection time.

Ask yourself:

· Did I reach my next step?

· Did I learn something new?

· Did I challenge myself?

If yes, check the box. Then, don’t break the chain. That’s it. Eventually, you’ll find that you’ll have 50 weeks chained together and skipping one is much harder.

Measuring your progress is important if you want to get positive emotional effects. Improvement in well-being, satisfaction, and happiness come from making progress towards your personal goals – tracking is the first step to making that progress visible.

As a last step in the CARHARTTGROW workbook review, click on each photo to learn more about how Jose, Amy, and Charles tracked their progress. Click Next to move on with the module.

So, what does a CARHARTTGROW conversation look and feel like between a Leader and an Associate?

Let’s check it out!

Choose a role – Leader or Associate. Once you choose, you will take on that role and “interact” with a conversation.

Amy is taking part in CARHARTTGROW to progress at work and make time to compete in the 1/2 marathon.

To get started, Amy takes the CARHARTTGROW training course, downloads the workbook and begins to fill it out.

She documents her priorities and values – what is her next step? Choose an option to continue the CARHARTTGROW process.

As a Leader, what is Jose's first step in the CARHARTTGROW process?

Select all that apply.
Jose receives a meeting request from Amy to discuss her CARHARTTGROW plan. What should he do?
Before the meeting with Amy, how should Jose prepare?
True or False: Jose can require Amy to share all her priorities and goals with him.

How can Jose help Amy?

Since Amy asked for the meeting, she comes with an agenda. She shares her Work priority with Jose and her plan to accomplish her goals.

Jose thinks about ways to help her reach her goal to learn more about the organization. What are some ideas to help Amy?
Alright – Amy’s and Jose first CARHARTTGROW conversation is about to end – What’s the next step for Jose?

Jose commits to helping Amy by:

• Allowing for time to meet.

• Helping Amy overcome barriers to her progress.

• Providing introductions to others in the organization when

He accepts her 30 minute check in once a month for the rest of year.
Amy works through creating goals – is the following goal a S.M.A.R.T. goal?

After Amy solidifies her goals, she looks for resources to help her reach them. She develops a list of items that she thinks will help her reach her goal.

Amy knows that the CARHARTTGROW process is Associate driven, so what’s her next step?
Amy reaches out to her Leader to discuss her CARHARTTGROW plan and sets up a meeting. How should she prepare?
True or False: It is required that Amy share all her growth plans with her Leader.
During Amy’s meeting, her manager suggests attending spotlights to learn more about the business. Jose asks if she would like to explore more options. Amy is very shy – help Amy respond.

Amy accepts her Leaders help with introductions and shows her him her tracker. He works with her to refine the timing from quarterly to monthly to ensure she is tracking measurable progress.

What is her next step?

Amy sets up her check in meetings and over the course of the year, she shares her progress with Jose.

Jose follows through on his commitment to help Amy, and he shares the feedback that he receives from others in the organization.

Amy shares her successes and barriers – so they can adjust her growth goals if needed.

Amy completes meeting with all suggested people and discovers that she is very interested in moving up in another department.

She is applying for new positions with her Leader’s help. She also completed the 1/2 marathon with her best time and raised 3000 dollars for relay for life.

At the end of the year, Amy and Jose meet again with a new CARHARTTGROW workbook that Amy fills out to help her attain her new growth goals.

Remember, CARHARTTGROW is voluntary, personal, flexible and driven by the Associate – it’s in your control.

You put in the time, you put in the effort. You make it as challenging or rewarding as you want.

Your growth journey is in your hands.

It’s time to start.

Need additional tools and support?

Reach out on Yammer with the tag #CARHARTTGROW to connect with others participating in this program.

Check out the CARHARTTGROW site on the portal for any updates.

Reach out to Carhartt Learning through email with specific questions or requests for additional learning.

If you need to download the CARHARTTGROW worksheet, click the resources tab or go to the CARHARTTGROW site.

Thank you for taking the time and effort to complete this course.

In order to receive credit, complete the survey by clicking the button below. Your response will be confidentially recorded and saved to your learning history.
